title: Node HTTP Primitives, Connections, Protocols, Timeouts, and Shutdown · Inference Brief: Node Request Response Streams
summary: A Node server inherits defaults without understanding header, request, keep-alive, upgrade, idle-socket, HTTP/2, or graceful-close behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts node request response streams increment in Node HTTP server protocol and timeout budget. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace IncomingMessage and ServerResponse as streams with header commitment, body ownership, backpressure, abort, error, and close lifetimes.
Test methods against retries, duplicate delivery, stale validators, HEAD, OPTIONS, changed state, and durable effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 7700 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate protocol support with explicit header, request, and drain policy; keep peak memory below the stated byte ceiling; then replace an average latency claim with p95 and event-loop saturation evidence.
